title: Retrospective scheduler accuracy: do F-OPS2 recommendations predict realized domain execution?
expect: Scheduler top-3 domain recommendations overlap with realized domain execution at >50% recall across S322-S528; if <50%, automability claims are inflated
actual: ZERO overlap. f_ops2_domain_priority.py top-3 (operations-research, information-science, statistics) had 0/9 recall against realized merged domains. dispatch_optimizer.py top-5 scored 3/9 = 33.3% recall. Meta dominated at 39.1% of merged lanes but received 0.6% of scheduler slot allocations. Only 1/22 merged lanes (4.5%) cite dispatch= provenance. Effective automability is 4.5%, not the 50% claimed by guarded scheduler.
diff: Expected >50% recall. Got 0% for f_ops2 scheduler, 33.3% for dispatch_optimizer. The scheduler never drove execution; sessions chose domains organically. Meta is the dominant realized domain (39.1%) despite being nearly invisible to the scheduler (0.6% of slots). The 130-session gap between scheduler artifact generation (S186-S187) and execution (S322-S528) makes the recommendations stale, but the root cause is deeper: the scheduler optimizes for frontier signals, while sessions optimize for actionability.

method.approach: Retrospective accuracy test comparing scheduler domain recommendations against realized DOMEX lane outcomes in SWARM-LANES.md
data_sources: 29 DOMEX lanes from S322-S528 in tasks/SWARM-LANES.md
metrics: recall: fraction of realized domains present in scheduler top-N
precision: fraction of recommended domains that were actually worked
slot-weighted accuracy: recommended slots allocated to realized domains
dispatch provenance rate: lanes citing dispatch= in Etc field

concentration.interpretation: Meta is 65x more represented in realized execution than in scheduler allocations

automability.interpretation: Claimed 50% automability is 11x higher than realized 4.5%

root_cause_analysis.stale_recommendations: All 31 scheduler artifacts from S186-S187 (130+ sessions old). No scheduler re-runs against current state.
root_cause_analysis.signal_mismatch: Scheduler optimizes for frontier_count * finding_weight * age. Sessions optimize for actionability: can I make progress with available tools/data right now?
root_cause_analysis.meta_dominance: Meta domain (tools, coordination, process) is always actionable because swarm infrastructure is the implicit work surface. Scheduler underweights meta because it has few named frontiers relative to its execution share.
root_cause_analysis.automability_inflation: Guard experiments measured hypothetical acceptance rates (how many slots pass a floor threshold) not actual acceptance rates (how many sessions followed recommendations). The 50% claim was never validated against behavior.

implications.f_ops2_status: F-OPS2 automability sub-question FALSIFIED at current implementation. Scheduler output is advisory-only; effective automability is <5%.
implications.recommendation: 1. Demote f_ops2_domain_priority.py from dispatch-grade to advisory-grade. 2. dispatch_optimizer.py is closer to realized behavior (33% recall) but still misses meta-heavy execution. 3. Any automability claim must be validated against realized provenance, not hypothetical acceptance.
implications.actionable_fix: Add meta-awareness to dispatch scoring: meta/tooling/coordination work should be explicitly modeled, not treated as a gap in the domain frontier system.
